hero.subtitle: An AI toolkit that does the writing and design work for your YouTube videos — give an idea, get a winning title, full description, thumbnail concept, and 5 ideas for your next video.
hero.noCard: 5 free AI calls per month. No credit card.

features.heading: 4 features that save hours
features.sub: Everything a YouTube creator needs — in one place.

step1.desc: Google sign-in or magic email link.

step2.title: Drop in an idea
step2.desc: Describe your video, or tell us what you're stuck on.

step3.title: Get publish-ready output
step3.desc: Titles, description, thumbnail, ideas — in 15 seconds.

pricingTeaser.heading: Start free, upgrade when you want
pricingTeaser.sub: Free gives you 5 AI calls per month. Paid plans add channel sync, automated insights, and unlimited AI.

pricing.heading: Simple pricing. Pick what you need.
pricing.sub: No hidden trials. No threatening downgrades. Cancel anytime — from settings.

cancel.a: Settings → 'Subscription' → 'Cancel'. Cancellation takes effect at end of current billing period, you keep access until then.

budget.q: What if I exceed my AI calls?
budget.a: Free: paused until next month. Creator: 50 is a soft cap — if you're heavy, just upgrade to Pro.

upgrade.q: What if I need to upgrade mid-month?
upgrade.a: You only pay the prorated difference. Lemon Squeezy handles it automatically.

data.q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
data.a: Kept for 90 days post-cancellation — you can come back without losing things. After 90 days, deleted automatically.

ideas.title: Idea board
ideas.placeholder: What just hit you? Write it raw — refine later.

image.needsBucket: Create a Supabase Storage bucket named idea-images

develop.title: Develop with AI
develop.subtitle: An agent that helps turn your idea into a script / caption / storyboard
develop.errorBudget: Monthly budget reached. Conversations resume next month.
develop.emptyHint: The agent will write the first message in a moment
develop.messagesLimit: Conversation reached its limit — close and start a new one if needed

empty.title: Space for your ideas
empty.subtitle: Every win started with one thought. Begin with a question:

prompts.p2: What do people ask you most?
prompts.p3: What do you wish someone had told you a year ago?

why.whyPlaceholder: Write to yourself — no editing. This will return to you in reminders.
why.forWhomPlaceholder: Describe your audience, even if it's just one person you imagine.

reminders.denied: Permission denied. Change it in your browser settings.
reminders.notSupported: This browser does not support push notifications
reminders.testSent: Notification sent. Check that it arrived.
reminders.scheduledTodo: Auto-scheduling arrives in Milestone 6 (depends on hosting)

thumbnail.title: Thumbnail Concepts
thumbnail.subtitle: 3 distinct thumbnail concepts — each using a different pattern (contrast face / before-after / numbered / object focus / red circle), with the exact text overlay, expression, color palette, and composition. Thumbnails drive 60-80% of YouTube CTR.

explainerBullets.ctr: YouTube's own research: thumbnails account for 60-80% of CTR
explainerBullets.patterns: 5 patterns that work in 2026: contrast face, before/after, numbered, object focus, red circle
explainerBullets.mobile: 70% of viewers are on mobile — text must be readable at 180px wide
explainerBullets.abTest: Recommended: A/B test between two concepts — YouTube supports it natively in Studio

thumbnail.ideaPlaceholder: Describe the video: what it's about, the hook, what the viewer must see immediately. The more detail, the sharper the concepts.

title.title: YouTube Titles
title.subtitle: 5 title variations tailored to your video idea — each with a different angle (curiosity / authority / contrarian / story / data), length scoring, and SEO keywords. The title is 80% of YouTube CTR — this is the most important feature.

explainerBullets.lengthMatters: Sweet spot is 50-65 chars — too short = lose SEO, too long = truncated on mobile
explainerBullets.angles: 5 different angles = 5 experiments — pick the best one or A/B test
explainerBullets.seo: YouTube is a search engine: keywords must be at the front
explainerBullets.abTest: Pro tip: publish with the strongest 2 titles, swap after 24h if CTR drops

title.ideaPlaceholder: Describe the video: what it's about, what the audience learns or sees, the main hook. The more detail, the sharper the titles.

findNext.title: Find your next video
findNext.subtitle: Stuck and not sure what to create now? Answer 5 short questions and AI will return a strategic direction, 5 concrete video ideas you can shoot tomorrow, and 2-3 tips tailored to exactly what's blocking you.

explainerBullets.stuck: Creators who have ideas but don't know what to focus on right now
explainerBullets.specific: 5 ideas with a real hook to say — not 'do a tutorial'
explainerBullets.tailored: Every idea is reasoned for *you* — based on the creator you are
explainerBullets.fast: Result in 15 seconds, not 15 strategy sessions

niche.label: What's your content niche?
niche.placeholder: Fitness & body recomposition · Investing for beginners · Home cooking · ...

audience.label: Who is your target audience?
audience.placeholder: Students aged 18-25 just starting · Creators at the beginning of their journey · ...

worked.label: What worked well for you recently?
worked.placeholder: Reel about a mistake I made starting out. Personal storytelling. Short clips.

blocks.label: What's blocking you / what's not working?
blocks.placeholder: Not sure if I should keep doing educational or pivot to personal. Views are dropping.

copy.title: YouTube Description
copy.subtitle: An AI agent that writes a ready-to-publish YouTube description (250-400 words), 3 alternative opening lines (the above-the-fold preview), 10-15 SEO tags, and a pinned comment — from your raw idea, in your tone, in your language.

explainerBullets.speed: Raw idea → full upload-ready description in 15 seconds
explainerBullets.options: 3 hooks = 3 different attempts at what shows above 'Show more'
explainerBullets.memory: When you mark 'not a fit' with a reason, the agent remembers and improves on every next call
explainerBullets.hashtags: SEO tags, not IG/TikTok hashtags. Pinned comment that sparks real engagement.

copy.ideaPlaceholder: What's the video? Content, context, or an idea from your idea board.
copy.feedbackSubtitle: Explain in a sentence or two. We'll save it and feed it into every future generation.
copy.feedbackPlaceholder: e.g., "Too formal", "I don't use those emojis", "Doesn't connect to my audience"
copy.feedbackSaved: Feedback saved. The next generation will improve.
copy.blockedSubtitle: The app hit 100% of the monthly AI budget. The budget resets at the start of next month, or you can raise it in settings.
copy.warningBanner: Monthly budget is nearly used up ({percent}% used)

analyze.title: Video check
analyze.subtitle: Upload a raw video *before* uploading to YouTube — Claude (AI Vision) analyzes it frame by frame and returns a full diagnostic: whether to upload at all, the best day & hour, whether it fits better as a Short or Long-form, how strong the hook is, how the video connects to your message, and concrete improvement tips. Your video never leaves your device — only frames are sent to AI.

explainerBullets.platform: Platform recommendation — where this video will land best
explainerBullets.timing: Optimal day and hour to publish
explainerBullets.hook: Hook quality score (strong / medium / weak) with reasoning
explainerBullets.message: Whether the video actually connects to your creator 'why'

analyze.uploadCta: Pick a video from your gallery
analyze.tooLarge: File is over 5GB. Compress it before uploading.
analyze.extractFailed: Couldn't extract frames from the video. The codec may not be supported.

budget.resetInfo: Budget resets at the start of each month

swipes.title: Style

subtitle.videos: Paste links to YouTube Shorts (or YouTube videos) that resonate — the app learns your taste and feeds it back into idea generation.
subtitle.styles: Edit aesthetics — which style speaks to you?
subtitle.photos: Still posts and carousels — what is the vibe?

addLink.label: Paste a link
addLink.placeholder: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/... or any YouTube video link
addLink.errorFailed: Couldn't fetch this link. Try another.
addLink.hint: Best with YouTube Shorts. Instagram/TikTok will work too if they have a public preview.

queue.emptyHint: Paste a link above to add content to swipe on

swipes.tip: Swipe right = I want this. Left = not for me. Arrow keys also work.

mirror.title: This week
mirror.subtitle: What your tracked channels published this week, what you created, what is stuck — all in one view.

empty.title: The mirror is still filling up

prompts.p1: What style feels most natural to you?
prompts.p2: Who are you as a creator, in 3 sentences?
prompts.p3: What do people say you're good at?

mirror.noContentThisWeek: Nothing this week yet. Then again — today still counts as this week.
mirror.noChannelUploads: No new uploads in your tracked channels this week. Add channels in "Channel analysis".

publish.title: Log a published post
publish.subtitle: Fill what's relevant — used in your weekly mirror and analytics

learn.subtitle: YouTube videos to help you grow as a creator
learn.empty: No videos found in this category
learn.savedEmpty: No saved videos yet. Mark ★ on anything useful.
learn.errorApi: YouTube fetch failed. Check your API key or daily quota.

analytics.title: Private metrics
analytics.subtitle: Enter the numbers the public YouTube API does not expose — CTR, retention, watch time. Public stats like views and likes are fetched automatically on the "Channel analysis" page.

empty.title: No data to analyze yet
empty.subtitle: After you mark an idea as published, come here to log views, likes, and shares.

form.title: Performance metrics
form.subtitle: Enter the numbers from the platform. Update anytime.

charts.noData: Not enough data — log metrics for at least one post

analytics.todoIntegration: TODO: Auto integration with Instagram Graph + TikTok Display API — future milestone

competitor.title: Add a channel
competitor.subtitle: Paste a link, handle (@name), or YouTube channel ID

errors.ai_failed: Analysis failed. Try again in a moment.
errors.parse_failed: AI returned an unexpected format. Try again.

auth.title: Sign in
auth.subtitle: Continue with Google or get a magic link by email
auth.checkInbox: Open your email and click the link.

setup.title: Initial setup required
setup.missingAnthropic: Anthropic API key is missing from .env.local

wizard.title: Initial setup
wizard.subtitle: A few minutes and you're ready for real work. Anything already set up is marked ✓
wizard.restartHint: After updating .env.local — restart the dev server (Ctrl+C, then npm run dev)
wizard.refreshHint: Once all keys are entered and the server is back — refresh this page to see everything turn green

supabase.title: Supabase — Database + Auth
supabase.description: The foundation of the app. Stores your ideas, swipes, analytics. Free up to 500MB.
supabase.step1: Open supabase.com → New Project. Pick a name, region (Frankfurt or London recommended), set DB password
supabase.step2: Once project ready, go to SQL Editor → New query → paste all the SQL below → Run.
supabase.step3: Storage → create 2 private buckets: idea-images and videos
supabase.step4: Project Settings → API. Copy the 3 keys, paste into .env.local as shown below
supabase.step5: Authentication → URL Configuration. Add http://localhost:3000 to Site URL

anthropic.title: Anthropic — AI agents
anthropic.description: Powers the copy agent, video analysis, and idea-development chat. Budget capped at $50/month.
anthropic.step1: Open console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys → Create Key
anthropic.step2: Important: in Limits — set Spending Limit to $50 to never accidentally exceed
anthropic.step3: Copy the key (shown only once!) to .env.local

youtube.title: YouTube Data API — Learning library
youtube.description: Optional. Pulls educational videos from YouTube for the Learn page.
youtube.step1: Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Library. Enable YouTube Data API v3
youtube.step2: Credentials → Create Credentials → API Key. Copy to .env.local

openai.title: OpenAI Whisper — Voice transcription
openai.description: Optional. Enables voice recording of ideas (auto-transcribed to text).
openai.step1: platform.openai.com → API keys → Create new secret key

vapid.title: VAPID Keys — Push reminders
vapid.description: Optional. Required to send push notifications to the browser.
vapid.step1: Open a terminal in the project folder and run:
vapid.step2: Copy the two values shown to .env.local (NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY and VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY)

wizard.allDone: Everything is set up! Click here to go back to the app

demo.title: Demo mode
demo.subtitle: You're seeing sample data. Connect Supabase in .env.local to save for real
demo.saveBlocked: Demo mode — connect Supabase to save

offline.queued: Will save when you're back online
offline.queuedIdea: Idea saved locally — will sync automatically

channels.title: Channel analysis
channels.subtitle: Paste a YouTube link — yours or anyone's — and get public stats + AI insights on what is working, what is not, and what to do next. No OAuth.
channels.emptyState: No channels yet. Paste a link above to get started.
